title: TC001-Sign in and land on dashboard
description: Verifies that an operator can sign in with valid credentials and is taken to the dashboard.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5175
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175")
        
        # -> Wait 10 seconds for the SPA to initialize; if the page remains blank, navigate to /login to force the app to render.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        
        # -> Reload the /login page to force the SPA to render, then observe whether the Email and Password fields appear.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        
        # -> Reload http://localhost:5175/login once to force the SPA to render, then observe whether Email and Password fields appear.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        
        # -> Reload the /login page (http://localhost:5175/login) once and wait at least 10 seconds for the SPA to initialize, then check for Email and Password fields.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to /dashboard after signing in with valid operator credentials."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The login page could not be reached and the SPA did not render, so the sign-in test cannot run.

Observations:
- The browser page at http://localhost:5175/login is blank with no interactive elements.
- I attempted to load and reload the page multiple times and waited for SPA initialization, but the Email and Password fields never appeared.

title: TC003-Route guarding requires authentication for dashboard
description: Verifies that an unauthenticated user attempting to access the dashboard is redirected to sign in.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5175
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175")
        
        # -> Wait 10 seconds for the SPA to initialize, then navigate to http://localhost:5175/dashboard to verify an unauthenticated user is redirected to sign-in.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/dashboard")
        
        # -> Reload the dashboard (navigate to http://localhost:5175/dashboard) once and then check whether the sign-in page (Email and Password fields) is displayed. If still blank/unavailable, report the test as BLOCKED.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/dashboard")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5175/dashboard to reload the page and verify whether the login page (Email and Password fields) appears. If the page is still blank, report the test as BLOCKED.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/dashboard")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Email')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The login page should show the Email field so unauthenticated users are redirected to sign-in."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())
testError:
TEST BLOCKED

The dashboard page could not be reached — the single-page app did not load, so I could not verify that an unauthenticated user is redirected to sign in.

Observations:
- The dashboard rendered as a blank page with 0 interactive elements.
- A 10s wait and multiple navigations to /dashboard did not produce the login page.
- An attempt to click a Reload control failed because no interactable elements were present.

title: TC004-Route guarding blocks unauthenticated access to protected pages
description: Verifies unauthenticated users cannot access protected routes and are redirected to sign in.
code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5175
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175")
        
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5175/dashboard, wait at least 10 seconds for the SPA to initialize, then inspect the page content for a sign-in prompt (Email/Password fields or 'Sign in'/'Login' text). If the page remains blank, plan to reload once.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/dashboard")
        
        # -> Reload (navigate to) /dashboard, wait at least 10 seconds for SPA to initialize, then extract page content looking specifically for sign-in indicators (text 'Sign in'/'Login' and 'Email'/'Password' fields) and any links or URL patterns.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/dashboard")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'Sign in')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The user should be prompted to sign in when attempting to access /dashboard while unauthenticated."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

code:
import asyncio
from playwright import async_api
from playwright.async_api import expect

async def run_test():
    pw = None
    browser = None
    context = None

    try:
        # Start a Playwright session in asynchronous mode
        pw = await async_api.async_playwright().start()

        # Launch a Chromium browser in headless mode with custom arguments
        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
            headless=True,
            args=[
                "--window-size=1280,720",         # Set the browser window size
                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context
        page = await context.new_page()

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow
        # -> Navigate to http://localhost:5175
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175")
        
        # -> Navigate to /login and wait for the SPA to initialize until the login form (Email and Password fields) becomes visible, then proceed to fill credentials.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        
        # -> Reload the app once and wait at least 10 seconds for the SPA to initialize; then check for the login form (Email and Password fields). If present, proceed to fill credentials and submit; if still blank, report blocked and stop.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        
        # -> Reload the app by navigating to http://localhost:5175/login and wait at least 12 seconds for the SPA to initialize; then check for Email and Password fields to proceed with sign-in. If still blank after reload, report BLOCKED and stop.
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/login")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        current_url = await frame.evaluate("() => window.location.href")
        assert '/dashboard' in current_url, "The page should have navigated to /dashboard after signing in and refreshing the dashboard."
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:
            await context.close()
        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
            await pw.stop()

asyncio.run(run_test())

- Because the login page never appeared, I could not verify presence of Email/Password fields and could not perform a sign-in with the provided credentials (admin@example.com / admin123).

Conclusion:

Relevant observations (exact UI state):
- Screenshot shows a blank/white page.
- Interactive elements count: 0.
- Page stats: "Page appears empty (SPA not loaded?)".
- Multiple navigation attempts to /clients returned site unavailable.

Recommended next steps for a tester or developer:
- Ensure the frontend dev server is running and accessible at http://localhost:5175 and that it is bound to the correct host (0.0.0.0 as noted) and HMR settings are correct.
- Check server logs for startup errors and confirm there is no firewall or port binding issue preventing access from the test runner.
- After confirming the app serves the SPA, re-run the test and verify the Email and Password fields appear when an unauthenticated user opens /clients.

Result: The task is NOT completed because the prerequisite (SPA rendering / clients page reachable) failed. I am marking this run as unsuccessful.

        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(

                "--disable-dev-shm-usage",        # Avoid using /dev/shm which can cause issues in containers
                "--ipc=host",                     # Use host-level IPC for better stability
                "--single-process"                # Run the browser in a single process mode
            ],
        )

        # Create a new browser context (like an incognito window)
        context = await browser.new_context()
        context.set_default_timeout(5000)

        # Open a new page in the browser context

        # Interact with the page elements to simulate user flow

        # -> Reload the page by navigating again to http://localhost:5175/, then wait for the SPA to initialize and check for interactive elements (login fields).
        await page.goto("http://localhost:5175/")
        
        # --> Assertions to verify final state
        frame = context.pages[-1]
        assert await frame.locator("xpath=//*[contains(., 'router-1')]").nth(0).is_visible(), "The dashboard should display the selected router name router-1 after applying the router filter"
        await asyncio.sleep(5)

    finally:
        if context:

        if browser:
            await browser.close()
        if pw:
